summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of MAESTRO. Found and FIXED a critical seed_data._gen_series argument-order bug (drift was being passed as n, causing 500 on all asset/sensor detail and wizard streaming endpoints). After fix: 27/27 backend tests pass (incl. SSE streaming for Claude Sonnet 4.5 + GPT-5.2 with persistence). Frontend smoke + interactive flows pass: Overview KPIs/8 assets/feed/Quick Actions; Equipment health gauge + 4 sensor charts + recommendations; Wizard end-to-end LLM streaming with feedback thumbs; Alerts filters + acknowledge + 3x3 risk matrix; Logbook modal create flow; Knowledge list+preview; Reports list + PDF download confirmed (maestro-HRM-01-*.pdf).

ui_bugs.issue: data-testid='alert-stream' missing on container (filters + items work fine, so non-blocking)

integration_issues.issue: Typing 'bearing' in kb-search did not visibly reduce the 7-doc list (backend ?q=bearing filter works correctly per backend test). Frontend may be filtering by title only or not wired to API. Worth verifying client-side filter logic.

action_items: Verify Knowledge frontend search calls backend ?q= or filters list properly (currently both before/after show 7 docs).
Add data-testid='alert-stream' wrapper on the Alerts page list container (cosmetic — filters and ack already work).
Optional: in seed_data._gen_series, prefer keyword args at call sites for clarity (signature was fixed to (base, noise, drift, n)).
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: seed_data._gen_series had signature (base, noise, n=60, drift=0.0) but profiles unpacked tuples positionally as (base, noise, drift) — passing drift float into n parameter -> TypeError on range(n). Fixed by reordering signature to (base, noise, drift=0.0, n=60). This was breaking all sensor endpoints and RAG context for wizard.

server.py @ line ~441 — 'Replay prior messages into provider history for continuity' loop uses `break` after first user message; comment acknowledges simplification but multi-turn conversations will lose full history beyond first prefill. Recommend a proper persistent provider history or restating last N turns in system prompt.
ChatSendReq.model is a Literal restricted to two exact strings; if model names change this breaks silently with 422. Consider widening or centralizing model whitelist.
No rate limiting / size limits on chat message or logbook fields — fine for demo but worth noting for production.
Hot-loaded synthetic sensor data is regenerated every request with fresh random noise — frontend charts will jitter on refresh; intentional for 'live' simulation but document this.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows pass; 1 testid missing, 1 search filter behavior question)

test_credentials: N/A — app has no auth (single-user demo per problem statement)
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 8 assets/5 alerts/3 logbook/7 knowledge on startup via ensure_seed(). Tests created TEST_-prefixed logbook entries (not cleaned up — minor) and several wizard sessions named TEST_session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green. If retesting, focus on: (a) Knowledge frontend search behavior (does ?q= get called?), (b) multi-turn wizard conversation continuity (currently only first user message is prefilled into provider history), (c) test_reports created TEST_ entries persist in Mongo logbook collection; safe to leave or clean. backend_test.py is the canonical regression suite — run with: `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: seed_data.py `_gen_series(base, noise, n=60, drift=0.0)` signature did not match call-site tuple unpacking `_gen_series(*p['vib'])` where profile tuples were (base, noise, drift). Python bound the drift float (e.g. 0.04) to parameter `n`, then `range(n)` raised TypeError: 'float' object cannot be interpreted as an integer. This made GET /api/assets/{id}, /api/assets/{id}/sensors, and POST /api/wizard/chat/stream (which calls _build_context_for_asset -> _live_sensor_snapshot) all return 500. REPRODUCTION: curl GET /api/assets/ast-mot-12 before fix -> 500; backend log shows TypeError in seed_data.py:137. MITIGATION (applied): swapped signature to `_gen_series(base, noise, drift=0.0, n=60)` so positional unpack `(base, noise, drift)` works as intended. All 5 previously-failing tests now pass; frontend Equipment/Wizard pages now render charts and stream LLM tokens.
